description: Fix Retry-After header parsing in the Confluence plugin to use the SDK's parseRetryAfterMs utility instead of reimplementing it incorrectly.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/confluence && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Use SDK parseRetryAfterMs in Confluence API callers
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the Confluence plugin to correctly parse Retry-After headers in both seconds and HTTP-date formats so that AI agents receive valid retry timing metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: Both apiV2 (line ~136) and apiV1 (line ~204) in confluence-api.ts use parseRetryAfterMs from @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk instead of `Number(retryAfter) * 1000`
The import for parseRetryAfterMs is added to the existing @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk import line
The behavior for numeric Retry-After values is unchanged (still converts seconds to milliseconds)
HTTP-date format Retry-After values are correctly parsed into milliseconds-from-now instead of producing NaN
cd plugins/confluence && npm run build passes
cd plugins/confluence && npm run type-check passes
cd plugins/confluence && npm run lint passes
cd plugins/confluence && npm run format:check passes
